subject: Mathematics
title: Problem Solver
tagline: Solve real-life word problems using all four operations!

stages.name: Stage 1
stages.question: Zanele has R714 and spends R28 at the school tuck shop. How much money is left?
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is R686.

stages.name: Stage 2
stages.question: Aisha has R756 and spends R344 at the grocery store. How much money is left?
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is R412.

stages.name: Stage 3
stages.question: Naledi shares 30 sweets equally into 6 bags. How many sweets go in each bag?

stages.name: Stage 4
stages.question: Amahle shares 20 apples equally into 5 bags. How many apples go in each bag?

stages.name: Stage 5
stages.question: Thabo buys 3 sweets at the market stall, each costing R7. How much does Thabo spend in total?
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is R21.

stages.name: Stage 6
stages.question: Aisha buys 3 oranges at the school tuck shop, each costing R8. How much does Aisha spend in total?
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is R24.

stages.name: Stage 7
stages.question: Kagiso has R860 and spends R158 at the grocery store. How much money is left?
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is R702.

stages.name: Stage 8
stages.question: Bongani shares 9 apples equally into 3 bags. How many apples go in each bag?

stages.name: Stage 9
stages.question: Naledi buys 4 apples at the spaza shop, each costing R5. How much does Naledi spend in total?
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is R20.

stages.name: Stage 10
stages.question: Amahle shares 12 notebooks equally into 3 bags. How many notebooks go in each bag?
